preview_text:
The model rates Turkey as a slight favorite, assigning the home nation a 38% win probability against a 31% chance for the USA. A draw is nearly as likely as either outcome, sitting at 31%, suggesting the match has material potential to end level.

The model's win-loss spread is narrow across all three outcomes. Turkey's 7-point edge over the USA reflects a relatively balanced contest; neither team's victory would constitute a major upset by the model's assessment. The draw probability—equal to the USA's outright win chance—underscores the competitive nature expected on a neutral pitch.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices diverge sharply from the model. Both the market and Polymarket favor the USA substantially, at 52–53% win probability, while assigning Turkey only 25%. The gap is meaningful: the market backs the USA by roughly 20 percentage points more than the model does, while depressing Turkey's chances by a similar margin. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct.
